verdict_reasoning: ATG9A is a multi-pass integral membrane protein (4 TM helices per protomer, homotrimeric) — the only TM protein of the core autophagy machinery. Its dominant compartment is endomembrane: TGN, autophagy vesicles (ATG9 reservoirs/VTCs), recycling endosomes, and autophagosomes. However, a documented PM pool exists: surface biotinylation and super-resolution TIRF microscopy confirm ATG9A at the plasma membrane, with the PM fraction significantly amplified by PM damage, starvation, or EGF/EGFR stimulation (SNARE-dependent fusion mechanism). ATG9A also cycles through the PM via clathrin/AP2-mediated endocytosis. Topology places both N- and C-termini cytosolic with intraluminal loops non-cytosolic; when ATG9A is in the PM, the intraluminal loops face the extracellular space, but the dominant biological actor is the endomembrane/autophagic pool. Verdict: contextual / dual_localization (vesicular cycling to PM, quantitatively minor at baseline but robustly induced by cell-stress/PM-damage stimuli). No pMHC-only story; not germline-restricted; not lysosomal-exocytosis-specific.
